title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: LDxBlocks detects linkage disequilibrium (LD) blocks genome-wide SNP data extends pipeline haplotype reconstruction, diversity analysis, genomic prediction feature engineering. Two LD metrics available: Standard r² – fast, kinship correction, suitable 10 M+ markers unstructured populations. Kinship-adjusted rV² – corrects relatedness-induced LD inflation, livestock, inbred lines, family-based cohorts. See LD Metrics vignette statistical detail decision table. C++/Armadillo computational core makes algorithm approximately 40x faster original Big-LD implementation (compiled code) typical window sizes. vignette covers complete pipeline – reading genotype data, block detection, haplotype analysis, parameter tuning – using ldx_geno example dataset (120 individuals, 230 SNPs, 3 chromosomes, 9 simulated LD blocks).

what: Why LDxBlocks? Relationship to the original Big-LD
title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: LDxBlocks built clique-based segmentation algorithm Kim et al. (2018), introduced interval graph modelling LD bins principled alternative sliding-window approaches. mathematical core – CLQD bin assignment, maximum-weight independent set block construction, Bron-Kerbosch clique enumeration – preserved exactly. LDxBlocks extends foundation address three limitations original implementation become critical modern breeding genomics programmes.

previous_headings: Why LDxBlocks? Relationship to the original Big-LD
title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: original Big_LD() calls cor() inside boundary-scan loop per CLQD() call, matrix operations running R interpreter. 50,000-SNP chromosome default leng = 200 amounts approximately 150,000 small R-level matrix multiplications per chromosome. WGS rice panel 3 million SNPs across 12 chromosomes, original implementation take days workstation. LDxBlocks replaces critical paths seven compiled C++ functions: outer loop compute_r2_cpp() parallelised OpenMP. 1,500-SNP window 500 individuals, r² matrix computes milliseconds rather seconds.
code: # These calls are now C++/Armadillo + OpenMP -- not R loops compute_r2_cpp(geno, digits = -1L, n_threads = 8L)   # ~40x faster maf_filter_cpp(geno, maf_cut = 0.05)                  # ~10x faster boundary_scan_cpp(geno, start, end, half_w, threshold) # ~20x faster

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: original Big_LD() requires complete genotype matrix RAM detection begins. 10M-marker, 5,000-individual WGS dataset approximately 400 GB R double matrix – impossible workstation. LDxBlocks enforces strict never-full-genome memory model. six supported formats stream genotypes one chromosome window time:
code: # VCF auto-converts to GDS cache; detection streams one window at a time be     <- read_geno("wgs_panel.vcf.gz") blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(be, method = "r2", n_threads = 8L) # Peak RAM ~ n_samples x subSegmSize x 8 bytes = 60 MB for n=5000, w=1500

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: WGS data phased externally (Beagle, SHAPEIT, pedigree), load phased VCF first pass resulting list extract_haplotypes(). Phased mode detected automatically hap1/hap2 structure:
code: # Statistical phasing via Beagle 5.x phase_with_beagle(input_vcf = "raw.vcf.gz", out_prefix = "phased",                   nthreads = 8L) phased <- read_phased_vcf("phased.vcf.gz", min_maf = 0.05)  # Pedigree-based phasing ped    <- data.frame(id = c("off1","sire1","dam1"),                      sire = c("sire1", NA, NA),                      dam  = c("dam1",  NA, NA)) phased <- phase_with_pedigree(ldx_geno, pedigree = ped)  # Extract haplotypes — phased strings: "011|100" haps_p <- extract_haplotypes(phased, ldx_snp_info, blocks, min_snps = 5) head(haps_p[[1]])   # "011|100" format

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: GWAS results available, map significant markers onto LD blocks define QTL regions flag pleiotropic blocks:
code: # ldx_gwas has a `trait` column (TraitA / TraitB) enabling pleiotropic detection. # For single-trait GWAS simply omit trait_col — the function still works, # setting n_traits = 1 and pleiotropic = FALSE for all blocks. qtl <- define_qtl_regions(   gwas_results = ldx_gwas,   blocks       = blocks,   snp_info     = ldx_snp_info,   p_threshold  = 5e-8,   trait_col    = "trait"      # omit this argument for single-trait GWAS ) head(qtl[, c("block_id","CHR","n_snps_block","n_sig_markers",              "lead_snp","traits","pleiotropic")]) #>                block_id CHR n_snps_block n_sig_markers lead_snp traits #> 1 block_1_156776_181114   1           25             1   rs1070 TraitA #>   pleiotropic #> 1       FALSE subset(qtl, pleiotropic)      # blocks with hits from both TraitA and TraitB #>  [1] block_id      CHR           start_bp      end_bp        n_snps_block  #>  [6] n_sig_markers lead_snp      lead_p        traits        n_traits      #> [11] pleiotropic   #> <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names)

code: library(LDxBlocks)  # 1. Open streaming backend (VCF auto-converts to GDS cache on first call) be     <- read_geno("mydata.vcf.gz")  # 2. Block detection — chromosome-by-chromosome, gc() after each blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(be, method = "r2", CLQcut = 0.70, n_threads = 8L) summarise_blocks(blocks)  # 3. Haplotypes — pass backend directly: streams one chromosome at a time, #    full genome is NEVER loaded into RAM simultaneously. haps <- extract_haplotypes(be, be$snp_info, blocks, min_snps = 5) div  <- compute_haplotype_diversity(haps) write_haplotype_diversity(div, "diversity.csv")  feat <- build_haplotype_feature_matrix(haps, top_n = 5,                                         encoding = "additive_012",                                         scale_features = TRUE) write_haplotype_numeric(feat, "hap_matrix.csv") write_haplotype_hapmap(feat, "hap_matrix.hmp.txt")  close_backend(be)

what: Why scale matters: the original Big-LD limitation
title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
text: original Big_LD() function (Kim et al. 2018) accepts plain R numeric matrix – entire genotype dataset must RAM simultaneously. 500-individual panel 50,000 SNPs works fine. modern breeding programmes operating whole-genome sequencing data, numbers become untenable: 120 GB rice example motivating use case LDxBlocks: second_variant_calling_merged.snpsOnly.maf05.miss20.vcf.gz, 3M-SNP, 204-sample WGS panel original Big-LD require loading entire genotype matrix single block boundary computed.

what: How LDxBlocks solves it: never-full-genome memory model
title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
text: LDxBlocks enforces strict memory contract: full genotype matrix never held RAM , dataset size format. peak RAM formula file-backed backends : Compare naive approach: 204 x 3,000,000 x 8 = 4.9 GB just matrix, LD computation allocates additional memory. cases run_Big_LD_all_chr() calls read_chunk(backend, col_idx) obtain exactly SNP window needs, frees rm() gc(FALSE) moving next window, never accumulates one chromosome RAM point. Two file-backed backends handle -disk streaming: backends read-memory-mapped: point columns requested current read_chunk() call RAM.
code: RAM_peak ~ n_samples x subSegmSize x 8 bytes          = 204 x 1500 x 8 = 2.4 MB per window (3M-SNP rice panel)          = 5000 x 1500 x 8 = 60 MB per window (5000-sample cattle panel)

title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
text: Peak RAM file-backed backend: RAMpeak≈n×w×8 bytes\text{RAM}_{\text{peak}} \approx n \times w \times 8 \text{ bytes} nn = individuals ww = subSegmSize. n=5,000n = 5{,}000 default subSegmSize = 1500: 5,000×1,500×8=60 MB per window5{,}000 \times 1{,}500 \times 8 = 60 \text{ MB per window} Compare plain-matrix path: 5,000×10,000,000×8=400 GB5{,}000 \times 10{,}000{,}000 \times 8 = 400 \text{ GB}.

text: single important memory parameter. C++ r² kernel allocates n×wn \times w genotype window w×ww \times w LD matrix: large nn, keep subSegmSize 1,500 reduce . moderate nn, increasing 3,000–5,000 reduces GDS reads cost RAM.

title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
text: boundary-scan half-window SNPs. boundary_scan_cpp() scans 2 * leng columns per candidate cut position, cost scaling O(p×leng2)O(p \times \text{leng}^2) per chromosome. 500 k-SNP chromosome leng = 200 ~20 billion element comparisons. Reduce leng 50–100 dense panels:
code: # Dense WGS panel: reduce leng, increase subSegmSize blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(   be_gds,   method      = "r2",   CLQcut      = 0.70,   leng        = 50L,      # narrower boundary scan   subSegmSize = 3000L,    # fewer GDS reads per chromosome   n_threads   = 16L )

title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
text: OpenMP thread scaling efficient approximately 8–16 threads typical window sizes. Benchmark hardware:
code: times <- sapply(c(1L, 2L, 4L, 8L, 16L), function(nt) {   system.time(     run_Big_LD_all_chr(be_gds, method = "r2", CLQcut = 0.7,                         n_threads = nt, verbose = FALSE)   )["elapsed"] }) names(times) <- paste0(c(1,2,4,8,16), " threads") round(times, 1)

title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
text: large panels, extract haplotypes one chromosome time avoid loading full genome matrix: chromosome-specific analyses (e.g. single chromosome ):
code: be     <- read_geno("wgs_panel.gds") blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(be, method = "r2", CLQcut = 0.70,                               n_threads = 16L, verbose = FALSE)  # Pass the backend directly — extract_haplotypes() detects LDxBlocks_backend # and streams one chromosome at a time internally. # Each chromosome is loaded, processed, rm()'d and gc(FALSE) called before # the next chromosome. The full genome is NEVER in RAM simultaneously. haps <- extract_haplotypes(be, be$snp_info, blocks, min_snps = 5) div  <- compute_haplotype_diversity(haps)  # Feature matrix and output (haps is a list of strings, always small in RAM) feat <- build_haplotype_feature_matrix(haps, top_n = 5,                                         encoding = "additive_012",                                         scale_features = TRUE) write_haplotype_numeric(feat, "haplotype_matrix.csv") write_haplotype_hapmap(feat,  "haplotype_matrix.hmp.txt") write_haplotype_diversity(div, "haplotype_diversity.csv")  close_backend(be) # Filter blocks and extract by chromosome — backend still streams haps_chr1 <- extract_haplotypes(be, be$snp_info, blocks,                                  chr = "1", min_snps = 5)

title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
text: GDS handle errors Windows. SNPRelate GDS file handles fork-safe. Avoid FORK-based parallelism (parallel::mclapply()); use n_threads > 1 (OpenMP within single process) instead. BEDMatrix missing data. BEDMatrix codes missing genotypes NA. correctly handled compute_r2_cpp() via mean imputation per column. pre-imputation required. “fewer min_snps_chr SNPs” messages. Many reference genomes hundreds unplaced scaffolds handful markers. Set min_snps_chr = 100 higher skip automatically. --memory boundary scan. boundary_scan_cpp() runs RAM large chromosomes, reduce leng 30–50. rarely affects block quality large-block boundaries evident even narrow scan window. long chromosomes (> 2 M SNPs). panels > 2 M SNPs per chromosome, set subSegmSize = 500 leng = 30. algorithm make GDS reads use far less RAM per step.

what: The kinship problem in LD estimation
title: LD Metrics: Standard r² and Kinship-Adjusted rV²
text: standard squared Pearson correlation two SNP dosage vectors gjg_j gkg_k : rjk2=[Cov(gj,gk)Var(gj)Var(gk)]2r^2_{jk} = \left[\frac{\mathrm{Cov}(g_j, g_k)}   {\sqrt{\mathrm{Var}(g_j)\,\mathrm{Var}(g_k)}}\right]^2 estimator assumes nn individuals exchangeable — drawn independently population. structured related populations assumption fails. Two individuals share recent common ancestor carry correlated alleles loci, just loci true LD. sample covariance therefore picks genuine recombination-based LD kinship-induced allele sharing, inflating r2r^2 relative true population value. consequence block detection blocks drawn broadly: regions genuinely independent appear correlated sample, clique graph edges , singletons absorbed large blocks.

title: LD Metrics: Standard r² and Kinship-Adjusted rV²

title: LDxBlocks — Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: Linkage disequilibrium (LD) block detection foundational step modern genomic analyses. Knowing SNPs co-segregate unit determines GWAS results interpreted, haplotypes defined population genetics, genomic prediction models structured. Despite importance, implementations LD block detection suffer three problems limit usefulness practice. kinship problem. Classical LD estimators (r²) assume independence individuals. livestock, crop, family-based human cohorts assumption systematically violated. Cryptic relatedness inflates pairwise correlations, causing LD appear stronger blocks drawn broadly wrong places. kinship-adjusted squared correlation rV² (Mangin et al. 2012, Heredity 108:285-291) corrects whitening genotype matrix inverse square root genomic relationship matrix (GRM): rVjk2=[Cor(V−1/2Gj,V−1/2Gk)]2rV^2_{jk} = \left[\mathrm{Cor}(V^{-1/2} G_j,\; V^{-1/2} G_k)\right]^2 VV VanRaden (2008) GRM V−1/2V^{-1/2} Cholesky whitening factor. Every pairwise correlation Big-LD algorithm replaced rV², giving block boundaries reflect true recombination structure rather population-structure artefacts. scale problem. original Big-LD implementation (Kim et al. 2018) contains compiled code – every matrix operation runs R interpreter. modern whole-genome sequencing panels 2-10 million markers inner loops prohibitively slow, loading full genotype matrix detection impossible workstations. LDxBlocks addresses C++/Armadillo computational core compiled via Rcpp, OpenMP-parallelised LD computation, unified multi-format /O layer, strict never-full-genome memory model: full genotype matrix never loaded RAM simultaneously regardless dataset size format. pipeline gap. original Big-LD stops block boundaries. breeding programmes GWAS follow-studies immediate next questions : haplotypes exist within block? diverse ? blocks harbour QTLs? build genomic prediction model captures multi-locus block effects? questions require coherent downstream pipeline original implementation provide. LDxBlocks tools? Unlike PLINK --blocks, LDxBlocks uses clique-based Big-LD segmentation algorithm require pre-specified number blocks naturally handles complex LD patterns. also supports kinship correction (rV²) six input formats natively. Unlike original Big-LD R package, LDxBlocks compiled C++ core (approximately 40x faster typical window sizes), streams genotype data disk without loading full genome RAM, adds rV² kinship correction, handles singleton SNPs explicitly, extends pipeline haplotype analysis genomic prediction. Unlike LDstore2 LDpred2, LDxBlocks produces interpretable genomic intervals (start/end position, rsID) output — just summary statistics — making blocks immediately usable annotation, diversity analysis, region-based modelling. Unlike gpart (Bioconductor successor Big-LD), LDxBlocks integrates directly R-based genomic prediction workflows (rrBLUP, BGLR, ASReml-R) haplotype feature matrix module, supports automatic GDS conversion WGS-scale datasets, provides GWAS-driven parameter auto-tuning.

title: LDxBlocks — Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: LDxBlocks complete pipeline genome-wide LD block detection related structured populations, downstream haplotype analysis genomic prediction utilities. extends Big-LD clique-based segmentation algorithm Kim et al. (2018) 12 concrete improvements: Dual LD metric — standard r² (default, fast, kinship correction, suitable 10 M+ markers) kinship-adjusted rV² (structured related populations) selectable via single method = argument. C++/Armadillo core — seven compiled functions handle r² computation, adjacency matrix construction, MAF filtering, boundary scanning, sparse LD, column-wise correlation. full r² matrix 1,500-SNP window computes milliseconds rather seconds. OpenMP parallelism — outer loop compute_r2_cpp() parallelised across threads; count controlled n_threads =. Unified multi-format /O — read_geno() auto-detects reads numeric dosage CSV, HapMap, VCF/VCF.gz, SNPRelate GDS, PLINK BED/BIM/FAM, -memory R matrices single entry point common backend interface (LDxBlocks_backend). Never-full-genome memory model — full genotype matrix never held RAM format. Numeric dosage CSV read pre-allocated 50,000-row chunks (peak RAM = one chunk, 2× file). VCF HapMap auto-convert streaming GDS cache. GDS PLINK BED backends load SNP window per CLQD call. gc(FALSE) called chromosome prevent heap fragmentation across 20–30 chromosome passes. MAF filter C++ — maf_filter_cpp() runs single O(np) pass, handling NA imputation monomorphic detection simultaneously. C++ boundary scan — boundary_scan_cpp() replaces R inner loop subsegmentation step runs hundreds times per chromosome, eliminating interpreter overhead -called function pipeline. Sparse r² computation — compute_r2_sparse_cpp() computes pairwise r² SNP pairs within user-specified bp distance, avoiding O(p²) cost large sub-segments distant SNPs always threshold. Automatic parameter tuning — tune_LD_params() performs grid search CLQcut parameters, selecting combination minimises unassigned forced GWAS marker placements. Haplotype reconstruction — extract_haplotypes() constructs phase-free diploid allele strings per LD block individual, nearly 1:1 true haplotype classes within high-LD blocks. Diversity metrics — compute_haplotype_diversity() computes per-block richness, Nei’s expected heterozygosity, Shannon entropy, dominant haplotype frequency. Prediction feature matrix — build_haplotype_feature_matrix() converts haplotype strings numeric dosage columns GBLUP, BayesB, machine learning models, capturing multi-locus epistatic effects implicitly.

title: LDxBlocks — Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: Within sub-segment, undirected graph constructed SNPs nodes edges connect pairs r2≥τCLQr^2 \geq \tau_{\mathrm{CLQ}} (CLQcut, default 0.5). adjacency matrix built build_adj_matrix_cpp() single O(p²) compiled pass. text: seven compiled functions src/ld_core.cpp (428 lines, RcppArmadillo + OpenMP) replace expensive R operations: compute_r2_cpp() replaces stats::cov() + R arithmetic full r² matrix. Speedup pure R approximately 40× 1,500 × 1,500 window 500 individuals. outer loop OpenMP-parallelised; threads share pre-standardised matrix Z (zero-mean, unit-variance columns) write disjoint (j, k) element pairs. boundary_scan_cpp() replaces triple-nested R loop cutsequence.modi(). chromosome 50,000 SNPs leng = 200, eliminates approximately 150,000 small R-level matrix operations subsegmentation step otherwise perform. maf_filter_cpp() replaces apply(G, 2, ...) single compiled pass. Approximately 10× faster panels > 100,000 SNPs; handles NA mean imputation monomorphic detection loop. build_adj_matrix_cpp() replaces ifelse(LD >= cut, 1L, 0L) C++ write place, avoiding allocation intermediate logical matrix.

title: LDxBlocks — Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: LDxBlocks enforces strict memory contract: full genotype matrix never held RAM dataset size format. Numeric dosage CSV — Two-pass chunked reading following OptSLDP pattern (Akohoue et al. 2026): Pass 1 scans header counts rows zero data loading. single pre-allocated matrix filled 50,000-row chunks via successive data.table::fread() calls. Peak RAM = one chunk (2× file). gc(FALSE) called chunk. VCF HapMap — Auto-converted SNPRelate GDS cache first call (placed next source file). Subsequent calls reuse cache. access streaming via read_chunk(). GDS PLINK BED — read_chunk(backend, col_idx) called per sub-segment per chromosome. subSegmSize = 1500 50,000 SNPs per chromosome, approximately 33 disk reads per chromosome. read loads 1,500-column slice; rest genome remains disk. Chromosome loop — run_Big_LD_all_chr() extract_haplotypes(), chromosome extracted, processed, freed rm(), gc(FALSE) called next chromosome touched, preventing heap fragmentation accumulating across 20–30 chromosome passes.

title: LDxBlocks — Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: n_threads parameter controls number OpenMP threads compute_r2_cpp(). Thread scaling efficient approximately 8–16 threads typical window sizes (1,500 SNPs). large windows (subSegmSize = 5000+), higher thread counts remain beneficial:
code: # Auto-detect physical cores n_thr  <- parallel::detectCores(logical = FALSE) blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(be, n_threads = n_thr)

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) m <- 80; b1 <- 60; b2 <- 60 make_block <- function(m, size, p, flip = 0.03) {   seed_snp <- rbinom(m, 2, p)   M <- matrix(seed_snp, nrow = m, ncol = size)   for (j in seq_len(size)) {     idx <- sample.int(m, max(1L, floor(flip * m)))     M[idx, j] <- pmin(2L, pmax(0L, M[idx, j] + sample(c(-1L, 1L), length(idx), TRUE)))   }   M } G <- cbind(make_block(m, b1, 0.30), make_block(m, b2, 0.60)) colnames(G) <- paste0("rs", seq_len(ncol(G))) rownames(G) <- paste0("ind", seq_len(nrow(G))) pos <- c(seq(1, by = 1000, length.out = b1), seq(5e6, by = 1000, length.out = b2)) SNPinfo <- data.frame(SNP = colnames(G), POS = pos) blocks <- LDxBlocks:::Big_LD(G, SNPinfo, method = "r2", CLQcut = 0.6,                  leng = 30, subSegmSize = 120, verbose = FALSE) head(blocks) } # }

what: When to use r^2 vs rV^2
title: Compute Standard r^2 LD Matrix — compute_r2
text: r^2 Use large unstructured datasets (> 500 k markers), random     mating populations, whenever speed matters. standard estimator     inflated related populations (.e. -estimate LD)     usually leads slightly conservative (larger) blocks rather     catastrophically wrong ones. rV^2 Use highly structured / related populations (livestock,     inbred lines, family-based human cohorts) kinship inflation     meaningfully distort block boundaries. title: Example Genotype Matrix — ldx_geno
text: 200 SNPs divided across three chromosomes (70, 70, 60 SNPs respectively). chromosome contains three distinct LD blocks separated short stretches (5 SNPs) low-LD singletons 50 kb inter-block gap: Chromosome 1 Three blocks 25, 20, 25 SNPs. Within block, SNPs generated copying common founder haplotype applying random single-allele flips (flip rate 4%) SNP independently, producing strong within-block LD (r² > 0.7 pairs). -block SNPs simulated independently binomial distributions randomised allele frequencies produce near-zero LD.

text: geno_file Path genotype file. Supported formats: numeric dosage CSV (`.csv`), HapMap (`.hmp.txt`), VCF (`.vcf`, `.vcf.gz`), SNPRelate GDS (`.gds`), PLINK BED (`.bed`). Format detected automatically file extension. out_blocks Path LD block table CSV. Columns: `CHR`, `start`, `end`, `start.rsID`, `end.rsID`, `start.bp`, `end.bp`, `length_bp`, `length_snps`, `block_name`. out_diversity Path haplotype diversity table CSV. Columns: `block_id`, `n_ind`, `n_haplotypes`, ``, `Shannon`, `freq_dominant`. out_hap_matrix Path haplotype genotype matrix. Format controlled `hap_format`. See section **Haplotype genotype matrix**. hap_format Output format haplotype matrix: `"numeric"` (default) - CSV rows = individuals,     columns = haplotype alleles coded 0/2/NA. `"hapmap"` - HapMap format rows = haplotype alleles,     columns = individuals, nucleotide encoding. maf_cut Minimum minor allele frequency. SNPs threshold removed block detection. Default `0.05`. CLQcut r^2 threshold clique edges CLQD. Higher values produce tighter, smaller blocks. Default `0.5`. method LD metric: `"r2"` (default) `"rV2"` (requires kinship matrix; see `Big_LD()`). leng Boundary scan half-window SNPs. Default `200L`. Reduce 50-100 dense WGS panels. subSegmSize Maximum SNPs per CLQD sub-segment. Controls peak RAM: `subSegmSize x n_individuals x 8` bytes. Default `1500L`. n_threads OpenMP threads C++ LD kernel. Default `1L`. min_snps_chr Skip chromosomes fewer post-filter SNPs . Default `10L`. Increase skip unplaced scaffolds. min_snps_block Minimum SNPs per haplotype block. Blocks smaller excluded haplotype analysis. Default `3L`. top_n Number top haplotype alleles retain per block output matrix. Default `5L`. scale_hap_matrix Logical. `TRUE`, scale haplotype matrix columns zero mean unit variance writing. Useful GBLUP-style models. Default `FALSE`. chr Character vector chromosome names process. `NULL` (default) processes chromosomes. verbose Logical. Print timestamped progress. Default `TRUE`. clean_malformed Logical. Stream-clean input file reading removing lines whose column count match header. Needed files NGSEP variant callers. Default FALSE.

title: Auto-Tune LD Block Detection Parameters — tune_LD_params
text: Performs grid search Big_LD parameters selects combination minimises, order priority: Unassigned GWAS markers (markers falling block). Forced assignments (nearest-block fall-back). Number blocks (parsimony). Deviation target_bp_band (biological plausibility). prefer_perfect = TRUE (default), combinations achieving zero unassigned zero forced assignments prioritised among . selecting best parameter set, tune_LD_params runs run_Big_LD_all_chr chromosomes assigns every GWAS marker block, returning final blocks assignments.

title: Auto-Tune LD Block Detection Parameters — tune_LD_params

NULL (default), sensible four-point grid CLQcut used. chromosomes Optional character vector chromosome names include tuning. NULL uses chromosomes snp_info. target_bp_band Length-2 numeric vector: preferred median block size range base pairs. Default c(5e4, 5e5) (50 kb - 500 kb). parallel Logical. TRUE, uses future.apply::future_lapply parallelism (user must set future plan calling). Default FALSE. seed Integer seed reproducibility. Default NULL. prefer_perfect Logical. Give priority parameter sets zero unassigned / zero forced GWAS markers. Default TRUE. return_all_perfect Logical. Include table zero-zero combinations returned list. Default TRUE.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.0
text: read_geno(): removed pheno_ids parameter. Sample subsetting caller’s responsibility — use $sample_ids match externally. Removed read_pheno() align_geno_pheno() entirely. Phenotype handling part LD block detection functions algorithmic role. run_Big_LD_all_chr(): parameter rV2method renamed kin_method. digits default changed 6 -1 (rounding) throughout.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.0
text: read_phased_vcf(): read pre-phased VCF 0|1 GT fields; returns hap1/hap2 gamete matrices plus combined dosage. phase_with_beagle(): call Beagle 5.x statistical phasing WGS data; returns path phased VCF.gz. phase_with_pedigree(): Mendelian allele transmission phasing within parent-offspring trios; exact parents homozygous. unphase_to_dosage(): collapse phased gamete matrices back 0/1/2. Auto-detects phased vs unphased input list structure. Unphased mode: diploid string "012201" per individual per block. Phased mode: two-gamete string "011|100" per individual per block. Blocks strictly per-chromosome; cross-chromosome blocks architecturally impossible. New encoding = parameter: "additive_012" (default) "presence_02". Phased + "additive_012": true 0/1/2 allele counts per gamete. Unphased + "additive_012": 0 2 (1 identifiable without phase). "presence_02": 0/2 presence/absence kernel methods random forests. New min_freq = parameter drop rare haplotype allele columns. Maps significant GWAS markers onto LD blocks post-GWAS. Flags pleiotropic blocks (significant hits multiple traits). Implements haploblock-based QTL cataloguing approach Tong et al. (2024) Theor Appl Genet 137:274. write_haplotype_numeric(): CSV rows = individuals, columns = haplotype alleles (0/1/2 0/2); compatible rrBLUP, BGLR, ASReml-R. write_haplotype_hapmap(): HapMap tab-delimited format rows = haplotype alleles, columns = individuals; nucleotide encoding 0→HH, 1→HA, 2→AA, NA→NN; compatible TASSEL GAPIT. write_haplotype_diversity(): CSV per-block diversity metrics optional genome-wide mean summary row. End--end pipeline (run_ldx_pipeline()): single-call wrapper genotype file outputs (blocks, diversity, haplotype matrix).

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.0
text: Multi-format /O: read_geno() supports numeric dosage CSV, HapMap, VCF / bgzipped VCF, SeqArray GDS, PLINK BED/BIM/FAM, plain R matrices. GDS streaming backend: peak RAM proportional one window. PLINK BED backend: BEDMatrix-backed memory-mapped access. read_chunk(), close_backend(): unified accessor interface. run_Big_LD_all_chr() accepts LDxBlocks_backend directly. min_snps_chr parameter: skip scaffolds fewer SNPs (default 10). ldx_gwas example dataset now includes trait column ("TraitA" / "TraitB") demonstrate multi-trait pleiotropic block detection via define_qtl_regions(trait_col = "trait"). single-trait GWAS argument omitted entirely.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.0
text: boundary_scan_cpp(): C++ boundary scan replaces R inner loop. maf_filter_cpp(): combined MAF + monomorphic filter, single O(np) pass. build_adj_matrix_cpp(): -place adjacency construction. compute_r2_sparse_cpp(): sparse r² within bp window.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.0
text: Chunked pre-allocated numeric CSV reader (read_geno() numeric path): two-pass strategy — header scan Pass 1, fixed 50,000-row chunks fill single pre-allocated matrix Pass 2. Peak RAM = one chunk, 2× file. gc(FALSE) called chunk. Directly follows .read_dosage_chunked() pattern Akohoue et al. (2026) OptSLDP. VCF HapMap mandatory GDS auto-conversion: read_geno() VCF HapMap auto-converts SeqArray GDS cache (placed next source file) SeqArray available, transparently switching streaming GDS backend. Cache reused subsequent calls. Explicit per-chromosome gc() run_Big_LD_all_chr(): rm(geno_chr,   info_chr) gc(FALSE) called chromosome’s block detection completes, preventing heap fragmentation across 20–30 chromosome passes. Backend streaming path extract_haplotypes(): LDxBlocks_backend object passed (instead matrix), haplotypes extracted one chromosome time using read_chunk(). chromosome freed rm() gc(FALSE) next chromosome loaded. full genome never RAM simultaneously.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.0
text: test-basic.R: smoke tests via example data (230 SNPs, 3 chromosomes). test-cpp.R: property-based C++ kernel tests. test-io.R: /O format round-trip tests via tempfiles. test-haplotypes.R: haplotype extraction, diversity, QTL, feature matrix, three output writers.

title: LDxBlocks 0.2.0 (2025-04-07)
text: C++/Armadillo core: compute_r2_cpp(), compute_rV2_cpp(), maf_filter_cpp(), build_adj_matrix_cpp(), col_r2_cpp(), compute_r2_sparse_cpp(), boundary_scan_cpp(). OpenMP parallelism via n_threads parameter. Dual LD metric: method = "r2" "rV2". prepare_geno(), compute_ld().

title: LDxBlocks 0.2.0 (2025-04-07)
text: CLQD(): fixed re_idx tracking dense-core pre-pass. Big_LD(): vapply() used throughout type-stability.
